name: Split Screen
description: A local multiplayer camera layout that assigns each player a viewport, camera owner, safe-area rules, and UI scaling profile.
design_notes: Split screen is both camera and UI infrastructure. Define split_screen_layout_id, viewport_rect, per-player camera ownership, safe-area padding, local co-op settings, UI scaling, and performance budgets. The layout must preserve gameplay readability for each player rather than simply dividing the screen evenly.
design_purpose: Let multiple local players have independent camera views on one display.
Preserve player-specific UI, camera framing, and input ownership in couch co-op or versus play.
Support local multiplayer without requiring all players to stay inside one shared camera volume.
player_fantasy: Each player feels they have their own readable view and controls while sharing the same physical screen.

parameters.name: split_screen_layout_id
parameters.description: Layout definition used to assign player viewports.

parameters.name: viewport_rect
parameters.description: Normalized viewport rectangle assigned to one local player.

parameters.name: local_player_camera_id
parameters.description: Camera instance owned by a specific local player.

parameters.name: safe_area_padding_units
parameters.description: Padding reserved for edges, gutters, and platform overlays.

parameters.name: ui_scaling_profile_id
parameters.description: UI scale profile applied inside each viewport.

parameters.name: viewport_render_budget_ms
parameters.description: Frame budget allocated to each local camera render.

edge_cases: A player joins or leaves while another player is in a menu, cutscene, vehicle, or lock-on camera.
One viewport needs subtitles, prompts, or accessibility settings that differ from another local player.
Four-player layouts can make minimaps, text, and target indicators unreadable without UI scaling changes.
Performance can drop sharply because scene rendering and post effects run once per viewport.
common_bugs: Input focus goes to the wrong player after layout changes.
UI uses full-screen coordinates instead of viewport_rect and appears in another player’s view.
Camera effects such as shake, FOV, or post-process settings bleed between players.
Safe-area padding is applied twice in nested viewport containers.
balancing_notes: Small viewports reduce peripheral vision, so enemies, prompts, and hazards may need closer telegraphs.
Dynamic split/merge can improve screen space but may be disorienting without stable rules.
Competitive local play may require symmetric viewport layouts even when asymmetric layouts look better.
accessibility_notes: Allow per-player UI scale, subtitle visibility, camera shake, and color settings where possible.
Avoid tiny text and low-contrast markers in split layouts; test from couch distance.

implementation_notes.unity: Create one camera and UI canvas set per local player, each bound to a viewport_rect and local player id. Use URP/HDRP camera stacks carefully so post effects, shake, and UI overlays do not leak across viewports.
implementation_notes.godot: Use SubViewport or multiple Camera nodes with explicit player ownership. UI scenes should be scoped to the player viewport and read that player’s input map, safe area, and accessibility profile.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use local player splitscreen support or custom viewport layout with PlayerController-owned cameras. UMG widgets should be created per local player and scaled through a split-screen profile.
implementation_notes.web: Render each local camera into a viewport rectangle and clip UI to that rectangle. Canvas, Phaser, or Three.js should budget culling and post effects per viewport, not per full-screen pass.

relationships.reason: Split screen provides per-player viewports and safe-area constraints for local co-op sessions.
Per-player controls and prompts need to align with each split-screen viewport.
Screen shake should be isolated or scaled per viewport so one player event does not disorient every player.

example_games.title: Mario Kart 8 Deluxe
example_games.note: Uses split-screen layouts for local multiplayer racing.

example_games.title: Halo: Combat Evolved
example_games.note: Uses split-screen cameras for local co-op and versus play.
